what_it_does: This JSON file declares a lifecycle hook named SessionStart that, when the session event matches startup, resume, or clear, runs a Node CLI command to synchronize episodic memory in the background.
observations: Only one hook type is registered: SessionStart.
The matcher uses the regex/keyword pattern 'startup|resume|clear'.
The hook action is a single shell command invoking 'node .../cli/episodic-memory.js sync --background'.
The script path is resolved through '${PLUGIN_ROOT:-${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}}', an environment-variable fallback chain.
No other lifecycle events or hook actions are defined in this file.
standouts: Triggers episodic-memory synchronization on session startup/resume/clear.
Path to the CLI is externalized via PLUGIN_ROOT / CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT.
conventions: Command hook type that shells out to a Node script.
Environment-variable fallback for plugin root resolution.
concerns: The command depends on environment variables not defined in this file.
No visible error handling or logging for the background command.
No documentation of matcher syntax or SessionStart semantics.
Shell-style interpolation inside a JSON string may be brittle.
notes: A minimal session-lifecycle hook that shells out to an episodic-memory sync CLI in the background whenever a session starts, resumes, or clears.
questions: How and where are PLUGIN_ROOT and CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT set?
What component emits the SessionStart event and how is the matcher evaluated?
What happens if the command fails?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's path and content both indicate it configures recollection hooks; it does exactly that by binding a session-start event to an episodic-memory sync command.
intention_signals: Keep the episodic memory store in sync with the current session state by automatically running a background sync whenever a session begins, resumes, or is cleared.
contribution: Provides a declarative hook binding that connects session lifecycle events to an external episodic-memory synchronization command, using environment-resolved paths and background execution.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON hook configuration that runs an episodic-memory sync command in the background on SessionStart events matching startup, resume, or clear.
intention_for_embedding: To keep episodic memory synchronized at session boundaries by invoking an external CLI tool without blocking.
